name: python-xmp-toolkit

metadata.name: python-xmp-toolkit
metadata.summary: Python XMP Toolkit for working with metadata.

documentation:
# Python XMP Toolkit

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/python-xmp-toolkit/python-xmp-toolkit.png?branch=master
:target: https://travis-ci.org/python-xmp-toolkit/python-xmp-toolkit
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/python-xmp-toolkit/python-xmp-toolkit/badge.png?branch=master
:target: https://coveralls.io/r/python-xmp-toolkit/python-xmp-toolkit
```

```{image} https://pypip.in/v/python-xmp-toolkit/badge.png
:target: https://crate.io/packages/python-xmp-toolkit/
```

```{image} https://pypip.in/d/python-xmp-toolkit/badge.png
:target: https://crate.io/packages/python-xmp-toolkit/
```

Python XMP Toolkit is a library for working with XMP metadata, as well as
reading/writing XMP metadata stored in many different file formats.

Python XMP Toolkit is wrapping Exempi (using ctypes), a C/C++ XMP library
based on Adobe XMP Toolkit, ensuring that future updates to the XMP standard
are easily incorporated into the library with a minimum amount of work.

Python XMP Toolkit has been developed by:
: - ESA/Hubble - European Space Agency
  - ESO - European Southern Observatory
  - CRS4 - Centre for Advanced Studies, Research and Development in Sardinia

## Documentation

Documentation is available at \<<http://python-xmp-toolkit.readthedocs.org>> or can be build using Sphinx:

```
pip install Sphinx
python setup.py build_sphinx
```

## Testing

Running the tests are as simple as:

```
python setup.py test
```

or (to also show test coverage)

```
source run-coverage.sh
```

```{image} https://d2weczhvl823v0.cloudfront.net/python-xmp-toolkit/python-xmp-toolkit/trend.png
:alt: Bitdeli badge
:target: https://bitdeli.com/free
```

functions.name: libxmp.utils.object_to_dict
documentation:
Extracts all XMP data from a given XMPMeta instance organizing it into a
standard Python dictionary.

functions.name: libxmp.utils.file_to_dict
documentation:
Extracts all XMP data from a given file organizing it into a standard Python
dictionary.

```
param file_path

Path to file to open.

return

An empty dictionary if there's no valid XMP in the file passed as
an argument.
```

functions.name: libxmp.utils.terminate
documentation:
Terminate usage of library.

Normally function should not be called. Cases however might exists
where memory clean-up is needed, then this method may be called.

:::{warning}
After this function have been called, any call to methods in
libxmp will result in a crash of Python.
:::

functions.name: libxmp.consts.options_mask
documentation:
Creates the options bit mask for consumption by exempi C functions.

Example:

```
opt = consts.options_mask( consts.XMP_SERIAL_OPTIONS, **kwargs )
```

or:

```
opt = consts.options_mask( consts.XMP_SERIAL_OPTIONS, omit_packet_wrapper=True )
```

documentation: < No open option

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_OPEN_READ
documentation: < Open for read-only access.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_OPEN_FORUPDATE
documentation: < Open for reading and writing.

documentation: < Only the XMP is wanted, allows space/time optimizations.

documentation: < Require the use of a smart handler (Text files, PDF and Illustrator are incompatible with this option).

documentation: < Force packet scanning, don't use a smart handler.

documentation: Do not include an XML packet wrapper.

documentation: Create a read-only XML packet wapper.

documentation: Use a highly compact RDF syntax and layout.

documentation: Include typical space for a JPEG thumbnail in the padding if no xmp:Thumbnails property is present.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_SERIAL_EXACTPACKETLENGTH
documentation: The padding parameter provides the overall packet length.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_SERIAL_WRITEALIASCOMMENTS
documentation: Include XML comments for aliases.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_SERIAL_OMITALLFORMATTING
documentation: Omit all formatting whitespace.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_CLASSMASK
documentation: The low 8 bits are an enum of what data structure to iterate.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_PROPERTIES
documentation: Iterate the property tree of a TXMPMeta object.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_ALIASES
documentation: Iterate the global alias table. - XMP Toolkit and Exempi don't implement this option yet

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_NAMESPACES
documentation: Iterate the global namespace table. - XMP Toolkit and Exempi don't implement this option yet

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_JUSTCHILDREN
documentation: Just do the immediate children of the root, default is subtree.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_JUSTLEAFNODES
documentation: Just do the leaf nodes, default is all nodes in the subtree.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_JUSTLEAFNAME
documentation: Return just the leaf part of the path, default is the full path.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_INCLUDEALIASES
documentation: Include aliases, default is just actual properties.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_OMITQUALIFIERS
documentation: Omit all qualifiers.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_SKIPSUBTREE
documentation: Skip the subtree below the current node.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_ITER_SKIPSIBLINGS
documentation: Skip the subtree below and remaining siblings of the current node.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_VALUE_IS_URI
documentation: The value is a URI, use rdf:resource attribute.  DISCOURAGED

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_HAS_QUALIFIERS
documentation: The property has qualifiers,includes rdf:type and  xml:lang.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_IS_QUALIFIER
documentation: This is a qualifier, includes rdf:type and xml:lang. */

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_HAS_LANG
documentation: Implies XMP_PropHasQualifiers,  property has xml:lang.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_HAS_TYPE
documentation: Implies XMP_PropHasQualifiers,  property has rdf:type.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_VALUE_IS_STRUCT
documentation: The value is a structure with nested fields.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_VALUE_IS_ARRAY
documentation: The value is an array (RDF alt/bag/seq).

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_ARRAY_IS_UNORDERED
documentation: The item order does not matter.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_ARRAY_IS_ORDERED
documentation: Implies XMP_PropValueIsArray,item order matters.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_ARRAY_IS_ALT
documentation: Implies XMP_PropArrayIsOrdered,items are alternates.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_ARRAY_IS_ALTTEXT
documentation: Implies kXMP_PropArrayIsAlternate,items are localized text.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_IS_ALIAS
documentation: This property is an alias name for another property.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_HAS_ALIASES
documentation: This property is the base value for a set of aliases.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_IS_INTERNAL
documentation: This property is an "internal" property, owned by applications.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_IS_STABLE
documentation: This property is not derived from the document content.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_IS_DERIVED
documentation: This property is derived from the document content.

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_IS_SCHEMA
documentation: Returned by iterators - #define to avoid warnings - Not defined by Exempi

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_IS_SCHEMA
documentation: Returned by iterators - #define to avoid warnings - Not defined by Exempi

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_PROP_COMPOSITE_MASK
documentation: Is it simple or composite (array or struct)?

variables.name: libxmp.consts.XMP_IMPL_RESERVED_MASK
documentation: Reserved for transient use by the implementation.

modules.name: libxmp.files
documentation:
The Files module provides support for locating the XMP in a file, adding XMP to
a file, or updating the XMP in a file. It returns the entire XMP packet, the
core pacakage can then be used to manipulate the individual XMP properties.
{class}`XMPFiles` contains a number of "smart" file handlers that know how to
efficiently access the XMP in specific file formats. It also includes a
fallback packet scanner that can be used for unknown file formats.

methods.name: libxmp.files.XMPFiles.__del__
documentation: Free up the memory associated with the XMP file instance.

methods.name: libxmp.files.XMPFiles.open_file
documentation:
Open a given file and read XMP from file. File must be closed again with
{func}`close_file`

```
param str file_path

Path to file to open.

raises XMPError

in case of errors.
```

```
.. todo::
    Change signature into using kwargs to set option flag
```

methods.name: libxmp.files.XMPFiles.close_file
documentation:
Close file after use. XMP will not be written to file until
this method has been called.

```
param close_flags

One of the close flags

raises XMPError

in case of errors.
```

```
.. todo::
    Change signature into using kwargs to set option flag
```

methods.name: libxmp.files.XMPFiles.get_xmp
documentation:
Get XMP from file.

```
return

A new  instance.

raises XMPError

in case of errors.
```

methods.name: libxmp.files.XMPFiles.put_xmp
documentation:
Write XMPMeta object to file. See also {func}`can_put_xmp`.

```
param xmp_obj

An  object
```

methods.name: libxmp.files.XMPFiles.can_put_xmp
documentation:
Determine if XMP can be written into the file.

Determines if a given {class}`libxmp.core.XMPMeta` object can be
written into the file.

```
param xmp_obj

An  object

return

true if  object writeable to file.

rtype

bool
```

API for access to the "main" metadata in a file.

XMPFiles provides the API for the Exempi's File Handler component. This
provides convenient access to the main, or document level, XMP for a file.
The general model is to open a file, read and write the metadata, then
close the file. While open, portions of the file might be maintained in RAM
data structures. Memory usage can vary considerably depending on file
format and access options. The file may be opened for read-only or
read-write access, with typical exclusion for both modes.

Errors result in raising of an {exc}`libxmp.XMPError` exception.

```
keyword file_path

Path to file to open.
```

```
.. todo::
    Documentation
```

functions.name: libxmp.exempi._load_exempi
documentation: Loads exempi library.

functions.name: libxmp.exempi.append_array_item
documentation:
Append a value to the XMP property array in the XMP packet.

Wrapper for xmp_append_property_item library routine.

Parameters
----------
xmp : pointer
    The XMP packet.
schema : str
    The schema of the property.
name : str
    The name of the property.
array_options : unsigned int
    The option bits of the parent array.
value : str
    The value of the item to be appended.
option_bits : unsigned int
    Mask of options.

Raises
------
XMPError : if the corresponding library routine fails

functions.name: libxmp.exempi.copy
documentation:
Create a new XMP packet from an existing instance.

Wrapper for xmp_copy library routine.

Parameters
----------
xmp : XmpPtr instance.
    The XMP packet object.

Returns
-------
newxmp : XmpPtr instance.
    A copy of the XMP packet object.

functions.name: libxmp.exempi.files_can_put_xmp
documentation:
Wrapper for xmp_files_can_put_xmp library routine.

Parameters
----------
xfptr : file pointer
    File pointer
xmp : pointer
    The XMP packet.

Returns
-------
tf : bool
   True if the XMP packet can be written to the file.

functions.name: libxmp.exempi.files_check_file_format
documentation:
Check the file format of a file.

Wrapper for xmp_check_file_format library routine.

Parameters
----------
filename : str
    Path to file.

functions.name: libxmp.exempi.delete_localized_text
documentation:
Remove a property.

Wrapper for xmp_delete_localized_text library routine.

# Parameters

xmp

: The XMP packet.

schema

: The schema of the property.

name

: The name of the property.

generic_lang

: The generic language you may want. Can be None.

specific_lang

: The specific language.

# Raises

XMPError : if the corresponding library routine fails

:::{versionadded} 2.0.0
:::

Wrapper for xmp_delete_property library routine.

Parameters
----------
xmp : pointer
    The XMP packet.
schema : str
    The schema of the property.
name : str
    The name of the property.

Raises
------
XMPError : if the corresponding library routine fails

functions.name: libxmp.exempi.files_close
documentation:
Close an XMP file, flush the changes.

Wrapper for xmp_files_close library routine.

Parameters
----------
xfptr : file pointer
    File pointer
options : int
    the options mask for closing the file

functions.name: libxmp.exempi.check_error
documentation:
Set a generic function as the restype attribute of all exempi
functions that return a boolean value.  This way we do not have to check
for error status in each wrapping function and an exception will always be
appropriately raised.

Parameters
----------
success : bool
    Return value from library function indicating success or failure.

modules.name: libxmp.core
documentation:
A module for parsing, manipulating, and serializing XMP data. The core module
has no knowledge of files. The core API is provided by the {class}`XMPMeta` and
{class}`XMPIterator` classes.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

Observatory (ESA/ESO)

functions.name: libxmp.core._remove_bom
documentation: Remove BOM (byte order marker) from a string.

functions.name: libxmp.core._remove_trailing_whitespace
documentation:
Remove trailing white space.

There is a lot of white space between </x:xmpmeta> and <?xpacket end="w"?>,
but we don't need that for presentation purposes.

functions.name: libxmp.core._force_rdf_to_utf8
documentation: Force RDF to unicode on 2.7, removing BOM in the process.

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.get_property
documentation:
Retrieves property value.

This is the simplest property accessor: use this to retrieve the values
of top-level simple properties.

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI for the property; can be null or
the empty string if the first component of the prop_name path
contains a namespace prefix.

param str prop_name

The name of the property. Can be a general path
expression, must not be null or the empty string. The first
component can be a namespace prefix; if present without a schema_ns
value, the prefix specifies the namespace.

returns

The property's value if the property exists.

raises

IOError if exempi library routine fails.
```

```
.. todo:: Make get_property optionally return keywords describing
    property's options
```
Functions for getting property values

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.get_array_item
documentation:
Get an item from an array property.

Items are accessed by an integer index

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI for the property; can be null or
the empty string if the first component of the prop_name path
contains a namespace prefix.

param str array_prop_name

The name of the array property. Can be a
general path expression, must not be null or the empty string. The
first component can be a namespace prefix; if present without a
schema_ns value, the prefix specifies the namespace.

param int index

The 1-based index of the item.

raises

IOError if exempi library routine fails.
```

```
.. todo:: Make get_array_item optionally return keywords describing
    array item's options
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.set_property
documentation:
Creates or sets a property value.

The method takes optional keyword aguments that describe the property.
You can use these functions to create empty arrays and structs by
setting appropriate option flags. When you assign a value, all levels
of a struct that are implicit in the assignment are created if
necessary; append_array_item() implicitly creates the named array if
necessary.

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI; see get_property().

param str prop_name

The name of the property. Can be a general path
expression, must not be null or the empty string; see
get_property() for namespace prefix usage.

param str prop_value

The new item value.

param **kwargs

source:15: (WARNING/2) Inline strong start-string without end-string.

Optional keyword arguments describing the options;
must much an already existing option from consts.XMP_PROP_OPTIONS

raises

IOError if exempi library routine fails.
```
Functions for setting property values

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.set_array_item
documentation:
Creates or sets the value of an item within an array.

Items are accessed by an integer index, where the first item has index
1\. This function creates the item if necessary, but the array itself
must already exist: use append_array_item() to create arrays. A new
item is automatically appended if the index is the array size plus 1;
to insert a new item before or after an existing item, use kwargs.

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI; see get_property().

param str array_name

The name of the array property. Can be a
general path expression, must not be null or the empty string. The
first component can be a namespace prefix; if present without a
schema_ns value, the prefix specifies the namespace.

param int item_index

The 1-based index of the desired item.

param item_value

The new item value.

param **kwargs

source:16: (WARNING/2) Inline strong start-string without end-string.

Optional keywork arguments describing the array
type and insertion location for a new item.  The type, if
specified, must match the existing array type,
prop_array_is_ordered, prop_array_is_alternate, or
prop_array_is_alt_text. Default (no keyword parameters) matches the
existing array type.

raises

IOError if exempi library routine fails.
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.append_array_item
documentation:
Adds an item to an array, creating the array if necessary.

This function simplifies construction of an array by not requiring that
you pre-create an empty array. The array that is assigned is created
automatically if it does not yet exist. If the array exists, it must
have the form specified by the options. Each call appends a new item to
the array.

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI; see get_property().

param str array_name

The name of the array property. Can be a
general path expression, must not be null or the empty string. The
first component can be a namespace prefix; if present without a
schema_ns value, the prefix specifies the namespace.

param str item_value

The new item value.

param dict array_options

An optional dictionary of keywords from
XMP_PROP_OPTIONS describing the array type to create.

param **kwargs

source:17: (WARNING/2) Inline strong start-string without end-string.

Optional keyword arguments describing the item
type to create.
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.get_property_bool

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI; see get_property().

param str array_name

The name of the array property. Can be a
general path expression, must not be null or the empty string. The
first component can be a namespace prefix; if present without a
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.get_localized_text
documentation:
Returns information about a selected item in an alt-text array.

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI; see get_property().

param str alt_text_name

The name of the alt-text array. May be a
general path expression, must not be None or the empty string.  Has
the same namespace prefix usage as propName in GetProperty.

param str generic_lang

The name of the generic language as an RFC
3066 primary subtag. May be None or the empty string if no generic
language is wanted.

param str specific_lang

The name of the specific language as an RFC
3066 tag. Must not be null or the empty string.

raises

IOError if operation fails.

return

The property's value.
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.set_localized_text
documentation:
Creates or sets a localized text value.

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI; see get_property().

param str alt_text_name

The name of the property. Can be a general
path expression, must not be null or the empty string. The first
component can be a namespace prefix.

param str generic_lang

A valid generic language tag from RFC 3066
specification (i.e. en for English).  Passing "x" for a generic
language is allowed, but considered poor practice.  An empty string
may be specified.

param str specific_lang

A specific language tag from RFC 3066
specification (i.e en-US for US English).

param str prop_value

Item value

param **kwargs

source:14: (WARNING/2) Inline strong start-string without end-string.

Optional keyword arguments describing the
options; must much an already existing option from
consts.XMP_PROP_OPTIONS

raises

IOError if exempi library routine fails.
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.delete_localized_text
documentation:
Remove a localized property.

:param str schema_ns:   The namespace URI; see get_property().
:param str alt_text_name:  The name of the alt-text array. May be a
    general path expression, must not be None or the empty string.  Has
    the same namespace prefix usage as propName in GetProperty.
:param str generic_lang:  The name of the generic language as an RFC
    3066 primary subtag. May be null or the empty string if no generic
    language is wanted.
:param str specific_lang: The name of the specific language as an RFC
    3066 tag. Must not be null or the empty string.

:raises: XMPError if operation fails.
Functions for deleting and detecting properties.

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.delete_property
documentation:
Delete a property from XMP packet.

Deletes an XMP subtree rooted at a given property.  It is not an error
if the property does not exist.

:param str schema_ns: The namespace URI; see get_property().
:param str prop_name: The name of the property; see get_property().

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.does_property_exist
documentation:
Queries for existence of a property.

:param str schema_ns: The namespace URI; see get_property().
:param str prop_name: The name of the property; see get_property().

:returns: True if the property exists, False otherwise.

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.does_array_item_exist

Reports whether an item exists in an array.

```
param str schema_ns

The namespace URI; see get_property().

param str array_name

The name of the array; see get_property().

param str item

The name of the item.

return

True if item is in array, False otherwise

rtype

bool
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.parse_from_str
documentation:
Parses RDF from a string into a XMP object.

The input for parsing may be any valid Unicode encoding. ISO Latin-1 is
also recognized, but its use is strongly discouraged.

Note RDF string must contain an outermost <x:xmpmeta> object.

:param str xmp_packet_str: String to parse.
:param bool xmpmeta_wrap: Optional - If True, the string will be wrapped
    in an <x:xmpmeta> element.
:param str input_encoding: Optional - If `xmp_packet_str` is a 8-bit
    string, it will by default be assumed to be UTF-8 encoded.
:raises: IOError if operation fails.
Functions for parsing and serializing
These functions support parsing serialized RDF into an XMP object, and
serializing an XMP object into RDF.  Serialization is always as UTF-8.

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.serialize_and_format
documentation:
Serializes an XMPMeta object into a string as RDF.

Note, normally it is sufficient to use either `serialize_to_str` or
`serialize_to_unicode` unless you need high degree of control over the
serialization.

The specified parameters must be logically consistent, an exception is
raised if not. You cannot specify both `omit_packet_wrapper` along with
`read_only_packet`, `include_thumbnail_pad`, or `exact_packet_length`.

```
param int padding

The number of bytes of padding, useful for modifying
embedded XMP in place.

param str newlinechr

The new line character to use.

param str tabchr

The indentation character to use.

param int indent

The initial indentation level.

param bool omit_packet_wrapper

Do not include an XML packet wrapper.

param bool read_only_packet

Create a read-only XML packet wapper.

param bool use_compact_format

Use a highly compact RDF syntax and
layout.

param bool include_thumbnail_pad

Include typical space for a JPEG
thumbnail in the padding if no xmp:Thumbnails property is present.

param bool exact_packet_length

The padding parameter provides the
overall packet length.

param bool write_alias_comments

Include XML comments for aliases.

param bool omit_all_formatting

Omit all formatting whitespace.

return

XMPMeta object serialized into a string as RDF.

rtype

utf-8 string.
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.serialize_to_unicode
documentation:
Serializes an XMPMeta object into a Unicode string as RDF and format.
Note, this is wrapper around `serialize_to_str`.

The specified parameters must be logically consistent, an exception
is raised if not. You cannot specify both `omit_packet_wrapper` along
with `read_only_packet`, `include_thumbnail_pad`, or
`exact_packet_length`.

```
param int padding

The number of bytes of padding, useful for
modifying embedded XMP in place.

param bool omit_packet_wrapper

Do not include an XML packet wrapper.

param bool read_only_packet

Create a read-only XML packet wapper.

param bool use_compact_format

Use a highly compact RDF syntax and
layout.

param bool  include_thumbnail_pad

Include typical space for a JPEG
thumbnail in the padding if no xmp:Thumbnails property is present.

param bool exact_packet_length

The padding parameter provides the
overall packet length.

param bool write_alias_comments

Include XML comments for aliases.

param bool omit_all_formatting

Omit all formatting whitespace.

return

XMPMeta object serialized into a string as RDF.

rtype

 string.
```

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPMeta.serialize_to_str
documentation:
Serialize into a string (8-bit, UTF-8 encoded) as RDF and format.

:param int padding: The number of bytes of padding, useful for
    modifying embedded XMP in place.
:param bool omit_packet_wrapper: Do not include an XML packet wrapper.
:param bool read_only_packet: Create a read-only XML packet wapper.
:param bool use_compact_format: Use a highly compact RDF syntax and
    layout.
:param bool include_thumbnail_pad: Include typical space for a JPEG
    thumbnail in the padding if no xmp:Thumbnails property is present.
:param int exact_packet_length: The padding parameter provides the
    overall packet length.
:param bool write_alias_comments: Include XML comments for aliases.
:param bool omit_all_formatting: Omit all formatting whitespace.
:returns: `str` 8-bit string in UTF-8 encoding (ready to be written to
    a file).

methods.name: libxmp.core.XMPIterator.skip
documentation:
Skips some portion of the remaining iterations.

```
param **kwargs

source:3: (WARNING/2) Inline strong start-string without end-string.

Optional keyword parameters from XMP_SKIP_OPTIONS to
control the iteration

returns

None

rtype

NoneType
```

Provides means to iterate over a schema and properties.

XMPIterator provides a uniform means to iterate over the schema and
properties within an XMP object. It is implemented according to Python's
iterator protocol and it is the iterator for XMPMeta class.

```
param obj xmp_obj

an XMPMeta instance

param str schema_ns

Optional namespace URI to restrict the iteration.

param str prop_name

Optional property name to restrict the iteration.

param **kwargs

source:10: (WARNING/2) Inline strong start-string without end-string.

Optional keyword arguments from XMP_ITERATOR_OPTIONS

returns

an iterator for the given xmp_obj
```
